Operation 'GetOrdersLabelAsync' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. The current description lacks clarity on what the operation actually does (generates labels), who can use it (OBA customers), what inputs are required (order identifiers), what is returned (PDF file), and important side effects (potential document separation). The summary is somewhat clear but could be more action-oriented. Added context about authentication, parameter usage, and the workaround for document separation to improve AI agent interpretability and help agents understand when and how to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Return a single PDF file with generated label and/or associated document(s)
data.current_description: Reserved for OBA customers only The account "Label format" settings page will control the page format settings used to print the postage label and associated documents. Certain combinations of these settings may prevent associated documents from being printed together with the postage label within a single document. If this occurs the documentType option can be used in a separate call to print missing documents.
data.summary_suggestion: Generate and retrieve postage label PDF for orders
data.description_suggestion: Generates and retrieves a PDF file containing the postage label and associated documents for specified orders. Reserved for OBA customers only. The label format and page settings are controlled by the account's "Label format" settings. Note: Certain format combinations may result in associated documents being generated separately; use the documentType parameter to retrieve missing documents in separate calls if needed. Requires valid order identifiers and Bearer token authentication.
data.reasoning: The current description lacks clarity on what the operation actually does (generates labels), who can use it (OBA customers), what inputs are required (order identifiers), what is returned (PDF file), and important side effects (potential document separation). The summary is somewhat clear but could be more action-oriented. Added context about authentication, parameter usage, and the workaround for document separation to improve AI agent interpretability and help agents understand when and how to use this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current version information of the ChannelShipper & Royal Mail Public API. Returns version details including the API version number and related metadata. This operation requires no authentication and can be used to verify API availability and version compatibility.

message: Operation 'GetOrdersAsync' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Description is completely empty, leaving AI agents unable to understand input parameters, output structure, or pagination behavior. Added details about pagination support, authentication requirement, and clarified that this returns order summaries rather than full details.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of orders for the authenticated user. Supports filtering and sorting through query parameters (page, pageSize, and additional filter options). Returns a collection of order summaries with basic information including order identifiers, status, and timestamps. Useful for browsing orders without full details.
data.reasoning: Description is completely empty, leaving AI agents unable to understand input parameters, output structure, or pagination behavior. Added details about pagination support, authentication requirement, and clarified that this returns order summaries rather than full details.

message: Operation 'CreateOrdersAsync' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description is empty, preventing AI agents from understanding the request structure, response format, or partial success behavior. Added context about batch processing capability, validation errors, and the side effect that orders become available for label generation.

data.description_suggestion: Creates one or more orders from the provided order data. Accepts a CreateOrdersRequest containing order details (recipient address, items, service type, etc.). Returns a CreateOrdersResponse with created order identifiers and any validation errors for failed orders. Partial success is possible - some orders may succeed while others fail validation. Created orders are immediately available for label generation.
data.reasoning: Description is empty, preventing AI agents from understanding the request structure, response format, or partial success behavior. Added context about batch processing capability, validation errors, and the side effect that orders become available for label generation.

Current description only mentions access restriction without explaining what the operation does. Added details about pagination, returned data structure, and clarified the distinction from the standard orders endpoint to help AI agents understand when to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Retrieve pageable list of orders with details
data.current_description: Reserved for ChannelShipper customers only - please visit ChannelShipper.com for more information
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of orders with complete details for ChannelShipper customers only. Supports filtering and sorting through query parameters (page, pageSize, and additional filter options). Returns comprehensive order information including recipient/sender addresses, item details, service specifications, and postal tracking information. Requires ChannelShipper account access. For Click & Drop customers, use the standard /orders endpoint.
data.reasoning: Current description only mentions access restriction without explaining what the operation does. Added details about pagination, returned data structure, and clarified the distinction from the standard orders endpoint to help AI agents understand when to use this operation.

message: Operation 'UpdateOrdersStatusAsync' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description is empty, leaving AI agents unable to understand the request format, status values, or effects of status changes. Added details about batch processing, error handling, and side effects on order workflow.

data.description_suggestion: Updates the status of one or more orders. Accepts an UpdateOrdersStatusRequest containing order identifiers and the new status value. Returns an UpdateOrderStatusResponse with confirmation of updated orders and any errors for orders that could not be updated. Status changes are immediately reflected in order records and may affect label generation eligibility or shipping workflow state.
data.reasoning: Description is empty, leaving AI agents unable to understand the request format, status values, or effects of status changes. Added details about batch processing, error handling, and side effects on order workflow.

message: Operation 'DeleteOrdersAsync' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, not_actionable. Current description buries critical information in unstructured text. Reorganized to clearly state the operation's purpose, input requirements, and severe side effects. Emphasized the financial and operational consequences of deleting orders with labels to ensure AI agents understand the gravity of this operation.

data.current_description: Reserved for ChannelShipper customers only - please visit ChannelShipper.com for more information Please be aware labels generated on orders which are deleted are no longer valid and must be destroyed. Cancelled label information is automatically shared with Royal Mail Revenue Protection, and should a cancelled label be identified on an item in the Royal Mail Network, you will be charged on your account and an additional handling fee applied.
data.description_suggestion: Deletes one or more orders by their identifiers for ChannelShipper customers only. Accepts orderIdentifiers parameter (comma-separated or array format). Returns confirmation of deleted orders. CRITICAL: Any labels previously generated for deleted orders become invalid and must be destroyed immediately. Cancelled labels are reported to Royal Mail Revenue Protection; if a cancelled label is found in the Royal Mail network, charges and handling fees will be applied to the account. Use with caution on orders with generated labels.
data.reasoning: Current description buries critical information in unstructured text. Reorganized to clearly state the operation's purpose, input requirements, and severe side effects. Emphasized the financial and operational consequences of deleting orders with labels to ensure AI agents understand the gravity of this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves one or more specific orders by their identifiers. Accepts orderIdentifiers parameter (comma-separated or array format). Returns a collection of order summaries with basic information including order status, recipient details, and timestamps. Use this operation when you need data for known order identifiers rather than browsing all orders.

data.current_summary: Retrieve details of the specific orders
data.current_description: Reserved for ChannelShipper customers only - please visit ChannelShipper.com for more information
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves complete details for one or more specific orders by their identifiers for ChannelShipper customers only. Accepts orderIdentifiers parameter (comma-separated or array format). Returns comprehensive order information including recipient/sender addresses, item details, service specifications, postal tracking information, and label status. Requires ChannelShipper account access. For Click & Drop customers, use the standard /orders/{orderIdentifiers} endpoint.
data.reasoning: Current description only mentions access restriction. Added details about input format, returned data structure, and clarified the distinction from the standard specific orders endpoint to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.
